headline: A US government order pulling Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 dominated the window alongside a cluster of 1,000-plus tokens per second inference releases.
body: Multiple channels report that a US government order forced Anthropic to withdraw Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 within three days, citing export-control concerns and a claimed jailbreak vulnerability, framing the move as a broader pattern of government control over frontier access. In parallel, two separate inference-speed milestones broke out: Xiaomi and TileRT's 1-trillion-parameter MoE model combining FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel, and Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma applying diffusion and noise prediction to text generation, both clearing 1,000 tokens per second on standard or H100 hardware. New entrants speculative decoding, persistent kernel, and noise prediction surfaced from these releases, while Model Context Protocol and design systems appeared via IBM's agentic alignment work. Knowledge graphs and prompt injection registered as technique breakouts through a self-improving agent memory build.
